summary: G003 integrates compliance/HQ AI governance: compliance now loads active policy_rules, sends them to deterministic and adapter-backed semantic review, merges semantic issues additively without weakening rule authority, fails closed when semantic review is unavailable, generates HQ daily briefings through the runtime AI adapter, and records provider calls with provider:model cost-log identity plus required usage/cost metering.

verification.command: npx biome check --write lib/ai/adapter.ts lib/ai/mockAdapter.ts lib/ai/providerAdapters.ts lib/ai/runtime.ts lib/logging/costLogger.ts lib/compliance/rules.ts lib/compliance/service.ts lib/hq/service.ts tests/p3-contract.test.ts tests/hq-screen-contract.test.ts tests/ai-provider-adapters.test.ts tests/p4-contract.test.ts
verification.evidence: Biome formatted/organized touched G003 files successfully.

verification.evidence: Focused G003-relevant Vitest run reported 4 files passed, 86 tests passed.
tsc --noEmit completed successfully after G003 fixes.
Full Vitest suite reported 10 files passed, 134 tests passed.

contractCoverage.evidence: mergeComplianceIssues appends semantic issues and ignores hostile semantic pass output when rule issues block export.
runComplianceCheck loads active policy_rules and passes them into evaluateCompliance and adapter.checkCompliance; baseline rules remain active when active policy rules are missing.
createDailyBriefing calls createRuntimeAIAdapter().generateDailyBriefing with company profile and opportunity memo context and persists AI output fields.
Provider adapters log provider:model identity, pipeline step, usage tokens, estimated cost, and reject missing logging/metering outside test paths.
